text.title: decision-tree-build-vs-buy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decision-tree-build-vs-buy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommends build without computing a 3-year TCO comparison against commercial alternatives.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Build decisions systematically undercount maintenance (15-20%/year of build cost), security audits, and opportunity cost. A formal TCO comparison must be run for any decision exceeding $50k.

failure-mode.description: Agent treats "strategic importance" as automatically meaning "build" — skips checking whether commercial solutions adequately cover the use case.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Even strategically important capabilities can use commercial foundations with custom extensions. Auth is strategically important but Auth0 handles it adequately for most companies.

failure-mode.description: Agent ignores vendor dependency risk when recommending a buy, presenting the choice as unconditional.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vendor lock-in, price increases, and acquisition risks are real. The recommendation must include a mitigation (standard protocols, data export capability, escrow) when dependency risk is non-trivial.
